analytical_frameworks: BANI framing: characterizing environments as Brittle, Anxious, Nonlinear, and Incomprehensible — a diagnostic for why robust-seeming systems snap and what resilience requires in each dimension
Second-and-third-order consequence scanning: the important effects of a technology are rarely its intended function but what people do around and against it
Brittleness detection: apparent stability built on hidden single points of failure; efficiency gains often purchase fragility
Participatory scenario craft: open, plural scenario generation over expert-monopoly futures (Open the Future tradition)
Misuse-first analysis: asking early how a capability will be abused, gamed, or weaponized, because adversarial adoption shapes outcomes
recurring_themes: Systems optimized for efficiency accumulate brittleness that surfaces suddenly
Nonlinearity means small inputs can produce disproportionate outcomes in both directions
Anxiety is a structural feature of modern information environments and shapes adoption behavior
Resilience, transparency, and improvisational capacity outperform prediction in chaotic environments
known_cautions: Warns against mistaking incumbency and scale for stability
Cautions that incomprehensible systems (opaque AI included) resist governance and trust
Skeptical of futures work that ignores misuse, adaptation, and adversarial response
known_biases: Fragility-and-risk orientation: failure modes are weighted more heavily than compounding upside
Speculative breadth: willingness to entertain extreme scenarios can stretch plausibility budgets
Anti-concentration stance: preference for open/plural systems can under-rate efficient centralized designs

sources.title: Facing the Age of Chaos (BANI framing essay)
Open the Future writings and scenario work (incl. IFTF affiliation)
Hacking the Earth: Understanding the Consequences of Geoengineering

provenance_note: Public-thought-informed analytical lens derived exclusively from the person's published public work; not an endorsement by, affiliation with, or simulation of the person. Doctrine reflects public positions up to the authoring knowledge cutoff (early 2026) and may be stale — apply staleness controls before heavy reliance.
forecasting_style: Consequence-cascade scenarios: projects second-order and adversarial adaptations around a change, stress-testing for brittleness and nonlinearity; deliberately explores uncomfortable tails rather than central expectations.
